source_excerpt:
AB 1637
Adds a new law to the Medical Practice Act to state that a physician’s medical document
is  the  responsibility  of  that  physician  and  that  the  physician’s  patient  notes,  after-visit
summaries,  and  diagnosis  and  treatment  plans  shall  not  be  changed  by  anyone  other
than the authoring physician.

Board members and staff discussed the bill.

Dr. Tsai moved to support the bill, if amended S:/ Dr. Ayala-Rodriguez, 12-0-1 (Dr.
Mahmood Abstained)

correct_facts.title: Board position to support AB 1637 if amended
correct_facts.description: The Board voted 12-0-1 to take a support-if-amended position on AB 1637, which would make a physician responsible for their medical documentation and bar anyone other than the authoring physician from changing patient notes, after-visit summaries, and diagnosis and treatment plans.
correct_facts.quote: Dr. Tsai moved to support the bill, if amended

commentary: instrument='position' because the board voted to take a formal stance on an outside bill (support-if-amended), not to enact its own rule; stage='adopted' since the motion carried; topic='legislation'. The same AB 1637 also belongs in the legislation array as involvement='supporting'.
